model.name: DeepSeek-V3 2024 release native FP8 W8A8 weight + logical absorbed BF16 MLA-KV deployment

metadata.checkpoint_variant: initial aligned DeepSeek-V3 release, not DeepSeek-V3-0324
metadata.architecture_summary: Sparse MoE causal decoder-only Transformer with 61 full-context MLA layers, three dense SwiGLU layers followed by 58 DeepSeekMoE layers, 256 routed experts with group-limited top-8 routing plus one always-active shared expert per MoE layer, auxiliary-loss-free sigmoid routing, YaRN-scaled RoPE, pre-RMSNorm, and untied input embedding and LM head. The release checkpoint also contains one MTP module, but ordinary one-token Decode excludes it.

weight_capacity.capacity_note: This is the physical dtype-aware payload for the 61-layer main model loaded by the selected ordinary Decode path. The pinned index metadata.total_size is a BF16-equivalent bookkeeping value and must not be used as native FP8 capacity.

metadata.parameter_count_convention: The engine executes all 61 MLA projection sets, all three dense FFNs, all 58 routers and shared experts, eight routed experts in each MoE layer, and the complete untied LM head for one Decode token. It therefore obtains exactly 36624596992 active matrix elements, matching the corrected official 36.6B convention after rounding. The input embedding contributes one BF16 row read rather than a full matrix multiplication. RMSNorm, RoPE, softmax, sigmoid routing, top-k selection, correction-bias arithmetic, expert weighting, and other elementwise work are outside matrix-parameter FLOPs.

precision_profile.unquantized_weight_dtype: BF16 for embedding, LM head, routers, and RMSNorm
precision_profile.activation_dtype: dynamic FP8 per-token-per-128-channel for selected W8A8 GEMMs; non-GEMM and core MLA operations use BF16 or implementation-specific FP32 accumulation
precision_profile.weight_precision_evidence: The initial release provides only native FP8 weights. Its quantization config specifies E4M3, dynamic activation quantization, and 128x128 weight blocks; every quantized matrix stores a corresponding FP32 weight_scale_inv tensor.
precision_profile.kv_precision_evidence: The pinned official demo sets the default dtype to BF16 and creates the absorbed 512-element kv_cache and 64-element pe_cache without an explicit dtype, so both persistent cache tensors are BF16. The release card's FP8-KV statement describes an optional SGLang capability, not the selected default path.
precision_profile.later_service_evidence_boundary: DeepSeek's February 2025 infrastructure disclosure reports FP8 matrix multiplication and dispatch with BF16 core MLA/combine in the online V3/R1 service. It corroborates the mixed-precision design but post-dates the 2024 release and does not identify KV storage dtype, so it is not used to infer BF16 KV.
precision_profile.quantization_metadata: FP32 weight_scale_inv is included in capacity metadata but excluded from engine weight traffic because schema v1 cannot model scale reads with the same batch and expert-union semantics without assigning false MAC FLOPs.

metadata.context_note: The official release advertises and evaluates 128K context, so max_context_tokens is 131072. The checkpoint config's max_position_embeddings=163840 is the YaRN internal position ceiling and is not treated as a separately demonstrated service context.
metadata.mtp_boundary: The release artifact contains one layer-61 MTP module, but the paper permits discarding MTP for ordinary autoregressive inference, the initial model card says community MTP support is still under development, and the official demo runs only the 61 main layers one token at a time. This baseline therefore excludes all MTP work and capacity. A future speculative profile needs acceptance, drafting, verification, and output-token normalization semantics and cannot be represented by simply adding one layer.
metadata.active_parameter_correction_note: The initial README_WEIGHTS stated 36.7B active and included both embedding and head. On 2025-01-24 the official documentation corrected the main-model value to 36.6B including only the 0.9B output head. The exact engine count of 36.624596992B follows that corrected convention; the historical correction does not alter the pinned 2024 weights.
metadata.checkpoint_size_pitfall: The pinned index metadata.total_size=1369062772000 equals two bytes times every tensor element, including FP8 weights and FP32 scales. It is a BF16-equivalent logical size, not the physical native-FP8 payload. The dtype-aware full-artifact payload is 688574839360 bytes, while the selected main-only deployment payload is 673150611808 bytes.
metadata.mla_note: The configuration models the absorbed MLA path: every layer retains one 512-element compressed KV latent and one 64-element decoupled RoPE key per historical token. It does not materialize expanded per-head K/V as persistent cache.
metadata.routing_note: The real router scores 256 experts with sigmoid, adds the learned correction bias for selection, first limits candidates to four of eight expert groups, selects eight experts, normalizes their scores, and scales them by 2.5. With no serving routing trace, batch expert traffic uses the engine's uniform-independent union estimate and does not reproduce group constraints, token correlations, load balancing, or expert-parallel placement.
metadata.embedding_note: Input embedding and LM head are not tied. Capacity includes both 129280-by-7168 BF16 matrices. Decode reads one 7168-element BF16 embedding row per request, while the full BF16 LM head is read once per scheduler step and amortized across the batch.
metadata.implementation_note: The logical-superchip baseline excludes FP8 scale traffic, dynamic activation quantization and dequantization, dtype-specific peak-FLOP accounting, expert-routing dispatch, tensor/expert-parallel communication, kernel padding, temporary activations, softmax traffic, and internal workspace materialization.
